title: Add a pull requests (open/closed) panel to a dashboard

blocks.content: In this milestone, you add a panel to your existing dashboard that displays GitHub pull requests data, including the count of open and closed pull requests over time.

Open the dashboard you created earlier. To enter edit mode, do one of the following:

- Click the **Edit** (pencil) icon in the top-right toolbar.
- Click the dashboard menu (three-dot icon) and select **Edit**.

Open the panel editor by doing one of the following:

- If you see an **Add visualization** button or placeholder, click it.
- If you see a **+** icon in the top toolbar, click it and select **Visualization**.

Either option opens the panel editor.
Click the **data source** dropdown and select your **GitHub** data source from the list.
In the query builder, click the **Query Type** dropdown and choose **Pull Requests**.

blocks.reftarget: [data-testid='data-testid Panel editor option pane field input Title']
blocks.content: In the **Panel options** on the right, enter `GitHub Pull Requests Overview` in the **Title** field.
Click **Save dashboard** to save the panel to your dashboard.
Your dashboard now includes comprehensive visualizations for both issues and pull requests, providing valuable insights into your repository's development activity and health.
